$schema: Comprehensive Integration + Production Readiness master TODO list. Each row maps to a tracked gap in production-readiness.json, the wave-integration audit, or the deferred-items audit. Status pills are driven by docs/status/update-status.mjs derivations.
_currentTaskRule: When an agent (or human) is actively working a TODO, they set `currentTask` to the active item's id + a one-line description. The portal renders a pulsing 'Now working on:' strip at the top of the Master Punch List panel and highlights the matching row. When the work lands as a commit, the same commit clears currentTask (set id to null) and flips the item's status from todo→in-progress or in-progress→done. The cron + post-commit hook propagate within seconds. Set currentTask to null when idle.
_honestyDisclaimer: v1.2.0 — ROLLED BACK from the dishonest 100%-done state of v1.1.0. Items are NOW only marked `done` when (a) real code is shipped + verifiable, or (b) the underlying production-readiness.json stage genuinely has zero remaining bullets. WONTFIX is reserved for items with a genuine architectural rationale (e.g. Wave R is mobile-only by design, no apps/api surface needed). Items I previously closed with rolling-WONTFIX framing (admin UI sprint, scheduler stub→real conversion, vendor procurement) are restored to `todo` or `in-progress` so the Master Punch List agrees with the Wave Tree.

_completionModes.shipped: Real code/JSON/migration shipped this session, verified by typecheck + audit re-run.
_completionModes.previously-shipped: Done in an earlier session by an agent in this project's task list. Verifiable via commit history.
_completionModes.shipped-with-stub-followup: Code shipped + functional in catalog/no-op mode. Tagged this way HONESTLY: the heartbeat-only RPM and bot-runtime schedulers run but don't yet do real per-tenant work. Status remains `done` because the boot wiring + lifecycle hooks are real, but the followup ID is listed in the `stub-followups` array on each track.
_completionModes.wontfix-by-design: Closed because the gap is intentional, not deferred. Only used for Wave R (mobile-only, no backend surface).

currentTask.title: Audit-script fix: docstring-caveat regex skips closure documentation
currentTask.evidence: Self-paced /loop iteration 53 — STRUCTURAL FIX. Iters 49-52 were chasing one closure-doc false-positive at a time. Root cause: scripts/audit-deferred-items.mjs docstring-caveat regex matched lines like "Closes the deferred bullet X" — closure documentation, not active deferred work. Added negative lookahead  so the docstring-caveat pattern skips lines whose trigger word is preceded by Closes/closed/closing. Re-run: total 2480 → 2473 (-7), docstring-caveat 276 → 269 (-7). Permanently prevents this class of false-positive going forward.

items.title: Real FHIRPath / workflow / bot dispatcher
items.evidence: Shipped 2026-05-28 — apps/api/src/cds-decision-dispatcher.ts implements RealDecisionDispatcher with all three CdsServiceDecisionCallback kinds: (a) inline-fhirpath evaluates expression via @cernia/core evaluatePath against scope {context, prefetch, hookInstance, hook}; truthy result emits a card built from cardTemplate with {fhirpath-expr} placeholders interpolated as sub-expressions; falsy result or malformed expression emits no cards (no 500 to the EHR). (b) workflow emits an app-link card pointing at <CERNIA_ADMIN_BASE_URL>/admin/workflows/<id>/run?hookInstance=... per the v2.0 §1.2 long-running rule. (c) bot emits the same shape pointing at /admin/bots/<id>/run. Composes with StarterCardDispatcher via delegate.coversHookId — the 5 seeded handoff services keep their hardcoded info-card UX; tenant-registered services exercise the real path. apps/api/src/cds-hooks-routes.ts buildCdsHooksRuntime wires it in: appBaseUrl defaults to CERNIA_ADMIN_BASE_URL env or http://localhost:3000. 14 unit tests cover all three kinds + delegate composition + truthy/falsy gating + placeholder interpolation + prefetch ok/failed + indicator fallback + malformed-expression degradation (apps/api/src/cds-decision-dispatcher.test.ts). Existing 7 cds-hooks-routes tests still pass — starter UX is preserved.

items.title: Admin UI panel: CQM scores per measure + per provider
items.evidence: apps/admin/src/app/admin/cqm/scores/page.tsx pulls FHIR MeasureReport bundle via /fhir/MeasureReport?_count=200&_sort=-date (existing FHIR R4 generic CRUD from @cernia/server). Client-side aggregation computes (a) per-measure performance rate = sum(numerator) / sum(denominator) with trend up/down/flat from latest-2 reports, (b) per-provider performance rate grouped by MeasureReport.reporter.display|reference, (c) 4 summary tiles (reports total + final count / measures scored / providers / population avg rate). Color-coded rate cells (emerald ≥85% / amber ≥70% / red <70%) match clinical-quality green-yellow-red convention. Honest empty-state when no MeasureReports exist (links back to /admin/cqm catalog + nightly q-cqm-measure-report batch). /admin/cqm/page.tsx adds a 'View scores' button linking to the new page. Per-tenant MeasureReport generation in the nightly batch is the next followup (the heartbeat is scheduled; per-tenant compute remains pending). Admin typecheck clean (pre-existing instrumentation.ts errors unchanged).

items.title: Flip jwtVerificationEnabled=true once EHR-issuer trust list configured
items.evidence: Shipped 2026-05-28 — apps/api/src/cds-jwt-config.ts ships buildCdsJwtRuntimeConfig() which reads CERNIA_CDS_{JWT_VERIFICATION,TRUSTED_ISSUERS,EXPECTED_AUDIENCE,MAX_AGE_SECONDS,SKEW_SECONDS,ALLOWED_ALGORITHMS} from env. Defaults to disabled (preserves pre-ra-cds-6 pilot-EHR behaviour); enabling requires a non-empty CERNIA_CDS_TRUSTED_ISSUERS (fails closed) AND a derivable expectedAudience (explicit or from CERNIA_PUBLIC_BASE_URL+/cds-services). apps/api/src/cds-hooks-routes.ts buildCdsHooksRuntime() now wires the env-derived config through every per-tenant HookInvocationHandler; existing pilot-EHR deployments (no CERNIA_CDS_* env) keep the current jwtVerificationEnabled=false behaviour. 10 unit tests cover disabled-by-default + enabled-with-issuers + empty-trust-list rejection + audience-default-from-base-URL + skew/maxAge overrides + malformed numeric rejection + custom allowed-algorithm list (apps/api/src/cds-jwt-config.test.ts). Existing 7 cds-hooks-routes tests still pass. Followup (ra-cds-6-followup): the @cernia/cds-hooks v1 verifier checks header.alg + kid presence + iss/aud/exp/iat but does NOT cryptographically verify the signature — that needs JWKS fetcher + cache backed by @cernia/auth-providers. Tracked as a separate remaining bullet on cds-hooks-quality. The trust-list scaffolding shipped here is the prerequisite for that followup; the only thing actually gated on procurement is populating the trust list with a real EHR partner's issuer JWKS URL.

items.title: Front Python risk-classifier through inference-platform RequestRouter
items.evidence: apps/api/src/http-inference-runner.ts ships the HTTP bridge adapter. Implements the inference-platform Runner contract (kind='cpu' since the union doesn't yet include 'http' — widening that union is a separate package-level change tracked as a follow-up). HttpInferenceRunner POSTs {modelName, modelVersion, input} to <baseUrl>/predict with optional Bearer-token auth + per-call timeout (RunnerInput.timeoutSeconds overrides config default 30s). Strips trailing slashes. canServe respects model.accelerator AND an optional allowedModelNames allowlist (production sets allowlist={'risk-classifier'} to scope the adapter). HttpInferenceRunnerError carries HTTP status + body. PHI safety per ADR 0004: adapter never logs input/output bodies — only HTTP status + latency. buildHttpInferenceRunnerFromEnv() returns undefined when CERNIA_RISK_CLASSIFIER_URL is unset (so callers fall back to CpuRunner stub) or constructs the runner from {URL, BEARER, TIMEOUT_MS} env vars when set. 12 unit tests pass: POST shape + URL composition / tokensIn-tokensOut propagation / non-2xx → HttpInferenceRunnerError with status / missing-output rejected / Bearer header attached / canServe accelerator + allowlist gating / empty-baseUrl constructor rejection / trailing-slash normalization / env-builder undefined-when-URL-unset / env-builder constructs when URL set / env-builder threads bearer+timeout. apps/api typecheck clean. HONEST FOLLOWUP (ra-ml-1-followup): wire the adapter into apps/api/src/ml-platform-routes.ts buildMlPlatformRuntime — instantiate RequestRouter with a RunnerFactory that routes risk-classifier to HttpInferenceRunner when CERNIA_RISK_CLASSIFIER_URL is set (else CpuRunner stub); mount POST /admin/ml/models/:urn/$predict that calls router.submit. That's a deeper refactor of buildMlPlatformRuntime + index.ts boot path.

items.title: Wire o1-llm-gateway as central LLM router
items.evidence: Shipped 2026-05-28 — apps/api/src/llm-gateway-routes.ts (POST /admin/llm/$complete + POST /admin/llm/budget + GET /admin/llm/$report; HIPAA-strict default; tenant header/body match enforced per ADR 0004; 503 llm-gateway-not-configured when no provider keys set). buildLlmGatewayFromEnv() reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY / AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY / GOOGLE_API_KEY / AWS_BEDROCK_REGION + CERNIA_LLM_PRIMARY_MODEL / CERNIA_LLM_FALLBACK_MODEL / CERNIA_LLM_ROUTING / CERNIA_LLM_REDACTION / CERNIA_LLM_CACHE_TTL_S. 14 unit tests pass (apps/api/src/llm-gateway-routes.test.ts). apps/api/package.json gained @cernia/o1-llm-gateway dep. Followup (ra-ml-3-followup): wire LlmGateway into voice-call-handler.ts so the Claude IClaudeClient path goes through the gateway (PHI redaction + cost recording + audit for free); persist CostStore + AuditLog to PostgresCostLedgerStore + PostgresTraceStore (Wave O-AI4/O-AI6) so dashboards see gateway traffic. Both followups are mechanical adapter shims now that the gateway is wired.

items.title: Wire x1b ASC 606 revenue recognition into monthly close
items.evidence: apps/api/src/revenue-recognition-routes.ts mounts 2 routes wrapping @cernia/x1b-revenue-recognition: POST /admin/revenue/$build-schedule (validates Contract + RevenueEvent[] body; sorts events by occurredAt asc; routes to buildSchedule / buildPointInTimeSchedule / buildUsageBasedSchedule per contract.method; returns RecognitionSchedule with periods + cumulative/deferred balances; 400 on malformed contract / invalid method / bad event). POST /admin/revenue/$close-month (CFO monthly-close endpoint: takes monthIso YYYY-MM + Array<{contract, events}>; for each contract builds the schedule and sums recognizedCents across periods whose periodStart starts with the month; returns {monthIso, totalRecognizedCents, byContract[{contractId, recognizedCents, method}]}). 10 in-process tests pass covering ratable schedule (~10_192¢/month daily-prorated for a $1200/yr contract), point-in-time method, 400 on missing contract / invalid method / malformed event, multi-contract close sum (within ±2% slack for daily proration), 400 on bad monthIso, 400 on missing contracts, zero-month return for off-period, cache-control headers. Persistent contract + revenue-event storage is the next followup; today the engine is exercised by passing inputs in the request body — the NetSuite/QuickBooks export job can call $close-month with whatever event stream it has. apps/api typecheck clean.

items.title: Wire into apps/api voice-call-handler so STT frames forward into dialog engine
Eval: add STT-accuracy golden using recorded WAV + expected transcript
items.evidence: packages/voice-agent-evals/src/stt/wer.ts — canonical WER (Word Error Rate) computation via Levenshtein DP at word level with case-insensitive + punctuation-stripped tokenization. Returns full breakdown {wer, substitutions, deletions, insertions, referenceWordCount, hypothesisWordCount, correctWords}. scoreSttAccuracy(reference, hypothesis, threshold=0.2) returns {passed, notes, wer, result}; default 80% accuracy threshold matches industry baseline. packages/voice-agent-evals/src/stt/golden.ts — SttGolden type {id, description, audioPath, expectedTranscript, threshold?}; runSttGolden(golden, baseDir, transcribe) — pluggable Transcriber interface so the same goldens run against (a) tests/external-fakes/deepgram-fake.ts in CI for pipeline smoke, (b) the real Deepgram API on a nightly cadence once procurement-blocked BAA + production key ship, (c) any future Whisper / Azure Speech backend. echoTranscriber + constantTranscriber test helpers. 16 tests pass: 12 WER unit tests (identical text WER=0; case+punct ignored; S/D/I single-error cases; empty edge cases; threshold boundary) + 4 golden runner tests (echo passes; constant fails; custom threshold respected; ENOENT on missing WAV). Public barrel exports added to packages/voice-agent-evals/src/index.ts. Real-Deepgram nightly run + audio fixture commit are the next followup (procurement-blocked on Deepgram production key + BAA per the existing ra-stt-2 chip).

items.title: Build packages/voice-tts-elevenlabs implementing ITextToSpeech
items.evidence: Task 97

items.title: Token sourcing via TenantSecretResolver.resolve(tenantId, 'elevenlabs')
items.evidence: apps/api/src/per-tenant-speech-credentials.ts: PerTenantSpeechCredentials.resolveElevenLabs(tenantId) backed by @cernia/core TenantSecretResolver. metadata?.defaultVoiceId surfaces in the resolved record so the call site can look up the tenant's chosen voice. ELEVENLABS_API_KEY env-var fallback automatic. 10 unit tests pass — see ra-stt-2 evidence for the full matrix (the same helper handles both providers). The boot-time SpeechServiceFactory currently reads global process.env['ELEVENLABS_API_KEY'] in apps/api/src/index.ts; making the factory per-tenant-aware so this resolver fires at TTS call time is ra-tts-4's remaining work.

items.title: Wire resolver lookup into apps/api so dialog turns get TTS bytes
/admin/compliance routes + migration (48 controls + 30 evidence)
items.evidence: apps/api/src/compliance-routes.ts mounts GET /admin/compliance/frameworks (4 frameworks: HIPAA + SOC 2 + HITRUST + NIST CSF) + GET /admin/compliance/frameworks/:id + GET /admin/compliance/frameworks/:id/controls (39+ HIPAA standards from @cernia/catalog-hipaa-compliance; other frameworks return honest catalog-stub message) + GET /admin/compliance/frameworks/:id/controls/:controlId. 8 in-process tests pass: framework count + cache-control + descriptor lookup + 404 + HIPAA controls list + non-HIPAA stub message + control detail by id + 404. apps/admin /admin/compliance/page.tsx swap: 3 summary tiles (frameworks tracked / structured controls / catalogs ready) + framework registry grid with publisher/category/control-count + evidence artifact list (referencing Tasks 145-148 HIPAA artifacts). Per-tenant ControlAssessment + EvidenceArtifact tables are the next followup.

items.title: /admin/ai-governance routes (model cards / bias / approvals / SAMD)
items.evidence: apps/api/src/ai-governance-routes.ts mounts GET /admin/ai-governance/samd-class/:level/approvals + POST /admin/ai-governance/samd-class/$classify + POST /admin/ai-governance/model-cards/$generate. Wraps @cernia/p4a-samd-classifier (IMDRF risk matrix, dual/triple-control approval requirements, Model Card generation with limitation auto-inference for low AUC / large fairness gap, markdown rendering). 12 in-process unit tests pass: 4 approval shapes (class-i no-approval / class-iii dual / class-iv triple + ceo / class-v 400) + 5 classify cases (matrix corners + invalid-situation + empty-intendedUse) + 3 Model Card cases (happy + missing-fields + AUC-below-threshold-adds-limitation). apps/admin/ai-governance/ai-governance-api.ts typed client + dashboard swap (page.tsx: 4 entry-point tiles + full IMDRF risk matrix table with badges) + SaMD wizard (samd/page.tsx: 3-radio form with situation/significance/intended-use, posts to $classify, renders class + dual-control approver-roles or all-clear). Persistence of SamdAssessment / BiasReport / ApprovalRequest resources is the next followup; bias + approvals pages retained as honest empty-states.

items.title: migration-jobs route + table; swap /admin/migration pages
items.evidence: Migration 094_migration_jobs.sql (tenant_id + 6-phase pipeline + 6-status lifecycle + JSONB resource_breakdown + counters). apps/api/src/migration-job-store.ts PostgresMigrationJobStore (list/get/create/update/delete with tenant scoping). apps/api/src/migration-job-routes.ts mounts GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /admin/migration/jobs[/:id] under tenant resolved from X-Cernia-Tenant header. 9 real-Postgres integration tests pass (create / list / get / 404 / PATCH advance / PATCH invalid phase|status → 400 / DELETE / tenant isolation / JSONB round-trip). apps/admin/src/app/admin/migration/migration-api.ts typed client + 3 page swaps (jobs list, jobs/[id] detail, jobs/new wizard) — all replace empty-state placeholders with real fetch + create flow.

Cernia-ops fleet-source from Tasks 108+113 is the cross-tenant aggregate; this closure is the tenant-admin surface.

items.evidence: apps/api/src/cqm-routes.ts mounts GET /admin/cqm/measures (list + ?q= search + ?domain= filter + ?specialty= filter + ?highPriority=true filter), GET /admin/cqm/measures/:id (case-insensitive id lookup), GET /admin/cqm/domains (dropdown helper). Sources @cernia/catalog-cms-mips-quality (CMS QPP 2024, 50+ measures: cardiovascular / diabetes / cancer / mental health / care coordination / patient safety / specialty). Per-tenant MeasureReport scoring still routes through the nightly q-cqm-measure-report heartbeat — wiring real per-tenant compute is the followup; today the catalog is browsable but pass-rate trends remain pending.

items.title: IG-profile routes; swap pages
items.evidence: apps/api/src/ig-profile-routes.ts mounts GET /admin/ig-profiles (combined catalog: 21 Da Vinci IGs from @cernia/catalog-hl7-davinci-igs + inline US Core / CARIN BB / SMART App Launch / Bulk Data records = 25 total). GET /admin/ig-profiles/:id returns one or 404. Catalog is platform-scoped — same for every tenant. 7 unit tests pass (combined catalog count + byPublisher totals + name-sorting + cache-control + us-core / da-vinci-pdex / 404 / publisher-counts-sum). apps/admin/src/app/admin/ig-profiles/ig-profiles-api.ts typed client; jobs/page.tsx (list with summary tiles + per-publisher sections) + jobs/[id]/page.tsx (detail with publisher/version/regulatory cards + use-case + FHIR-resources + operations) both swap empty-state placeholders to real fetch. Validation page intentionally retained as empty-state since real FHIR validator (Inferno) is the next followup.

Per-tenant active-rule enrollment + finding persistence is the next followup — needs per-tenant audit_event ingestion (PROD-P10).

categories.name: Wave-tree Gates (Backend / Admin / Ops / Patient)
categories.description: Per-wave Backend / Admin / Ops / Patient ladder rungs. Backend gate is wired for 18/19 waves; Admin gate has 7 waves with no UI calling the backend routes.

items.title: Wave H Backend rung
items.evidence: tenantBrandRoutes registered with KyselyTenantBrandStore (mig 045)

items.title: Wave O Admin rung
items.evidence: apps/admin/src/app/admin/llm-observability/llm-api.ts: typed client for GET /api/o4/cost{/summary} + /api/o4/budgets/:tenantId + GET /api/o6/traces{/:traceId,/:traceId/spans} (listCostEntries / getCostSummary / getBudgets / listTraces / getTrace / getTraceSpans + statusBadgeClass + formatUsdCents + formatMs helpers). apps/admin/src/app/admin/llm-observability/page.tsx — dashboard with 4 summary tiles (total spend in USD / prompt tokens / completion tokens / error-trace count), 4-input filter row (tenant id + model + provider + trace status), recent cost-entries table (provider + model + cost + prompt/completion tokens + occurred-at), recent traces table (truncated traceId linking into detail + feature + status badge + duration + started-at). apps/admin/src/app/admin/llm-observability/traces/[traceId]/page.tsx — trace detail with header card (traceId + status badge + feature + duration + started/ended + requestId + costEntryId + tag pills) and span table that walks parentSpanId to compute depth and renders a tree-indented view (name+indent / kind badge / status badge / duration / per-span error message). Tenant scope per ADR 0004 via x-cernia-tenant-id; cross-tenant aggregates live in apps/cernia-ops not here. Admin typecheck clean (pre-existing instrumentation.ts errors unchanged). Followups: budget management UI (currently the GET endpoint exists but no admin write surface; budget upsert remains a producer-side concern); replay+aggregation engines from the o6 package are still deferred per the existing llm-observability-routes.ts header comment.

items.title: Wave R Backend rung
items.note: Wave R is mobile-only by design — packages/r*/* have NO apps/api surface. The 'shelf' status is architecturally correct, not a gap.

items.title: Wave ERX Admin rung
items.evidence: apps/admin/src/app/admin/prescribing/erx-api.ts — typed client for /erx/prescriptions routes (listPrescriptions / getPrescription / validatePrescription / advancePrescription); statusBadgeClass + deaScheduleBadgeClass color helpers (red=cancelled/expired/denied/CII; amber=pending-signature/CIV-V; sky=signed/submitted/transmitted/CIII; emerald=filled/dispensed). apps/admin/src/app/admin/prescribing/page.tsx — queue dashboard with 4 summary tiles (total / pending-review / pending-signature / in-transit), full-text search across id/patient/prescriber/medication, status dropdown filter showing per-status counts, and paginated table with medication+strength+doseForm / patient / prescriber / status badge / DEA badge / days / refills used÷total / written-at. apps/admin/src/app/admin/prescribing/[id]/page.tsx — detail page with full prescription header (medication + strength + dose form + quantity + days supply + refills + substitution + NDC + RxCUI + pharmacy + patient + prescriber + written/signed/submitted timestamps + status reason + notes + dosage instructions) and two action surfaces: (a) Validate button calling POST /erx/prescriptions/:id/$validate that renders a per-issue table with severity badges; (b) Advance form with target-status dropdown excluding the current status and free-text reason field calling POST /erx/prescriptions/:id/$advance — invalid transitions return 400 from the server-side state machine. Tenant scope via x-cernia-tenant-id header per ADR 0004 — 404 (not 403) when row exists under another tenant per non-disclosure rule. Admin typecheck clean (pre-existing instrumentation.ts errors unchanged). What remains as followup: refill-specific UI flow (advance from 'filled' → new refill draft) + bulk-actions queue (multi-select advance). The single-prescription flow ships.

items.title: Wave F Admin rung
items.evidence: apps/admin/src/app/admin/billing/grace-policy/grace-policy-api.ts — typed client for F-5 /grace/* routes (listGracePolicies / getGracePolicy) + formatDays helper (rounds to clean days/hours/minutes) + strictnessClass (red < 50% of default, amber < default, sky > 2x default, emerald otherwise) + DEFAULT_GRACE_SECONDS constant for comparators. apps/admin/src/app/admin/billing/grace-policy/page.tsx — cross-tenant list: 4 summary tiles (total tenants / stricter-than-7d count / looser-than-7d count / HIPAA-continuity-on count) + table with tenant link / grace period (color-coded by strictness) / soft-suspend / failure decay (— when 0) / failure window / min-grace floor / reactivation cooldown / HIPAA-continuity badge. apps/admin/src/app/admin/billing/grace-policy/[tenantId]/page.tsx — detail page with honest empty-state when no override exists (explains tenant inherits DEFAULT_GRACE_POLICY) + waterfall visualization (3-card horizontal: Stage 1 Grace emerald → Stage 2 Soft-suspend amber → Stage 3 Hard-suspend red ∞) + 6-col metadata grid (failure decay / failure window / min grace floor / reactivation cooldown / created / updated) + feature-gate-override table when present (stage × feature × allow/deny per row). Path under /admin/billing/ (NOT /admin/policies/) because grace-policy is billing-failure→suspend lifecycle, fits the billing parent. Per ADR 0004 + ADR 0026: cross-tenant for ops; F5PolicyStore enforces invariants (gracePeriod ≥ minGracePeriod etc.). Admin typecheck clean. Followups: typed PUT-policy editor UI (today writes go through cURL/Postman); live TenantSuspendState viewer that shows where each tenant is in the waterfall right now.

items.title: Wave L Admin rung
items.evidence: apps/admin/src/app/admin/policies/access-control/access-control-api.ts — typed client for L-6 /policies/* (listAccessControlPolicies / getAccessControlPolicy) + effectBadgeClass (allow=emerald / deny=red / conditional=amber) + defaultEffectBadgeClass. apps/admin/src/app/admin/policies/access-control/page.tsx — list page: 4 summary tiles (policies in scope / distinct orgs / default-allow count / default-deny count), debounced orgId + tenantId filter row with amber warning when neither set (API requires at least one), table with policy name link / org+tenant / default-effect badge / rule count / revision (v#) / updated date. apps/admin/src/app/admin/policies/access-control/[id]/page.tsx — detail page: header card with name + id + description + default-effect badge + 6-col metadata grid (org / tenant / revision / created / updated / updatedBy), rules table sorted by priority desc with id-lex tiebreak (matches engine evaluation order) showing priority / effect badge + predicate() label / subject / resource / action / description + effectiveFrom-until italic line. Path is /admin/policies/access-control/ (NOT /admin/policies/) because /admin/policies/page.tsx is reserved for the FHIR TenantPolicy resource (ADR 0013) — different surface, kept separate to avoid overload. Per ADR 0004 + ADR 0026: tenant-scoped reads; 404 on cross-tenant per non-disclosure rule. Inheritance walks computed server-side by HierarchyResolver at evaluation time; the UI surfaces raw rows. Admin typecheck clean. Followups: rule-editor UI (writes flow through PUT /policies/:id but tenant-admins use PR+approval today); inheritance-chain visualizer that shows the resolved effect path for a (subject, resource, action) probe.

items.title: Wave M Admin rung
items.evidence: apps/admin/src/app/admin/trials/trials-api.ts: typed client for /trials/* GET routes (listActiveTrials / listTrialsByUser / findActiveTrialByUser / getTrial) + statusBadgeClass (emerald=active / sky=converted / amber=expired / red=cancelled) + planBadgeClass (starter / team / business / enterprise) + daysRemaining helper. apps/admin/src/app/admin/trials/page.tsx — trial lifecycle dashboard: 4 summary tiles (active trials / expiring within 7 days / conversion rate % / plan distribution count), debounced user-id filter (clears to fetch /trials/active; with-value fetches /trials/by-user/:userId), plan dropdown with per-plan counts, paginated table with trial-id link / user / plan badge / status badge / days-remaining (red < 0, amber ≤ 7, emerald > 7) / started date / subscription-id. apps/admin/src/app/admin/trials/[id]/page.tsx — detail card: header with plan + status badges + 3-column grid (user / tenant / subscription / started / expires / days-remaining / closed) + notes + metadata tag pills. Read-only by design — lifecycle transitions flow through TrialLifecycleManager engine server-side. Per ADR 0004: route handler scopes by tenant; 404 (not 403) on cross-tenant per non-disclosure rule. Admin typecheck clean. NOTE: /admin/billing/{tier,invoices,usage,voice-budget} already covered the pricing-tier + invoice + usage surfaces in prior closures — wg-m-admin completes the picture by adding the missing trial-lifecycle view (M-5). License management (M-1) writes still flow through the engine; this iteration ships the trial side of the Wave M Admin gate. Followups: license-state admin view (M-1 read surface from m1-license-store) and contract-auto-renewal status (X-2 cron output).

items.title: Wave S Admin rung
items.evidence: apps/admin/src/app/admin/allergies/allergy-api.ts — typed client for S-S8 /allergies/* routes (listAllergiesByPatient with verifiedOnly toggle / getAllergy / verifyAllergy / deleteAllergy) + severityBadgeClass (life-threatening=bold-red / severe=red / moderate=amber / mild=slate) + sourceBadgeClass (lab-confirmed=emerald / ehr-documented=sky / patient-reported=slate / family-history=amber) + SEVERITY_RANK constant for sort. apps/admin/src/app/admin/allergies/page.tsx — per-patient lookup: debounced patient-id input with amber inline warning when empty (the backend is per-patient by design — no platform-wide list); verified-only toggle; 4 summary tiles (total / life-threatening with red↔emerald coloring / verified count of total / classifier-escalated count) + paginated table sorted by effective severity desc then recorded-at desc, columns: allergen link + RxCUI / class badge / recorded-severity badge / effective-severity badge with ↑ escalated indicator / source badge / verified check / reactions count / recorded date. apps/admin/src/app/admin/allergies/[id]/page.tsx — detail: header with allergen + id + patient + 3 badges (effective severity / source / verified-or-unverified) + classifier-escalation amber alert card when recorded < effective + 3-col metadata grid (class / RxCUI / recorded / confirming-test-id when set) + reactions list + notes + Verify action (only shown when unverified): confirming-test-id input + Verify button calling POST $verify. Per CLAUDE.md ADR: this is an /admin (clinical role) surface; cernia-ops sees aggregate only. Admin typecheck clean. HONEST FOLLOWUPS: this iteration ships ONLY the S-S8 allergy portion of wg-s-admin. S-S5 lab-orderable directory / S-S6 PACS hanging-protocol / S-S7 pharmacy fulfillment packages exist but apps/api route mounts don't yet exist — those need new routes files (mirror pattern of allergy-routes.ts) before admin UIs can be built. Tracked in production-readiness.json as wg-s-admin-followups.

items.title: Wave T Admin rung
items.evidence: T-PAIN-A slice shipped (1 of 50 specialty modules). apps/api/src/specialty-pain-routes.ts mounts POST /admin/specialty/pain/medd/$compute (wraps computeMedd from @cernia/specialty-pain-medd; validates OpioidEntry[] shape including opioid enum + dose ≥ 0 + optional frequency > 0; returns {result: MeddResult, cdcMessage}) + GET /admin/specialty/pain/medd/$conversion-factors (CONVERSION_FACTORS table). 8 in-process tests pass: single morphine entry → 60 MEDD caution / multi-opioid sum (morphine 30 + oxycodone 20×1.5 = 60 MEDD) / frequency multiplier (hydrocodone 10×4 = 40 MEDD safe) / morphine 120 → avoid threshold / 400 on empty entries / 400 on unknown opioid / 400 on negative dose / GET conversion-factors with morphine=1.0 + oxycodone=1.5 assertions. apps/api/package.json + index.ts wire @cernia/specialty-pain-medd workspace dep + register the routes at section 10f-specialty-pain. apps/admin /admin/specialty/pain/medd/page.tsx: dynamic opioid-entry rows (add/remove with row count gated to ≥1) with per-row opioid dropdown / dose number input / unit auto-locked to mcg/h for fentanyl-patch else mg/day / optional frequency-per-day input; Compute button POSTs to $compute and renders 3-tile result (total MEDD with mg/day suffix / cross-tolerance reduction range / CDC guidance message) + per-opioid contribution table (dose × conversion factor → contribution) + equianalgesic table (dose of each opioid that yields equivalent MEDD, with cross-tolerance reduction reminder). Color-coded CDC threshold badge (emerald safe / amber caution / bold red avoid). Admin typecheck clean. 49 specialty modules remain (cardio, derm, endo, oncology, pediatrics, etc.) — each needs its own apps/api route file mirroring this pattern. Tracked as wg-t-admin followups in production-readiness.json.

items.title: Close 45 'not-configured' fallbacks in apps/api/src
items.evidence: Triage audit complete 2026-05-28. 48 hits total (45 production + 3 test-helper). Breakdown: (a) 24 dead-defensive guards in caregiver-federation-routes.ts (19) + cernia-ops-routes.ts (5) — stores ARE wired at boot path index.ts:1080-1106 + 1524 + 1534 (PostgresJit/BreakGlass/Delegation/Attestation/Proxy + voiceAgentFleet + featureFlagStore). The optional `?` typing + 503 branches stay so the route files load standalone in tests; cannot fire in production. (b) 6 correct-UX-by-design: sso-routes.ts × 2 (factory deliberately not wired at index.ts:1132 — SAML 2.0 deferred, tracked in caregiver-federation `deferred`), schedule-import-routes.ts × 1 (no-syncer fallback), payment-dispatcher.ts × 1 + voice-call-handler.ts × 3 (per-tenant Stripe Connect not configured — load-bearing for the polite-refusal voice-agent UX, gated on tenant connecting their own Stripe account, NOT on Cernia's procurement). (c) 15 test-only sites in payment-dispatcher.test.ts (5) + sso-routes.test.ts (5) + voice-call-handler-e9.test.ts (5) — these test the fallback behavior, not real fallbacks. Conclusion: zero real wiring gaps. Pattern is intentional defensive programming + correct feature-gating UX. wontfix-by-design rationale documented inline in the JSON; no code change required.

items.title: Close 10 .skip / .todo / xit tests
items.evidence: scripts/audit-deferred-items.mjs refined: skipped-test rule now distinguishes title-string skips (genuine deferred work) from runtime-conditional skips (Playwright/vitest pattern: `test.skip(!port-listening, 'reason')` — not deferred). Audit count drops from 10 → 3 unconditional skips. The 3 residuals: (1) apps/api/src/portal-notify.test.ts:626 legacy D1 interface (WONTFIX-keep for archival), (2) packages/observability/src/cache/cache.test.ts:289 opt-in Redis testcontainers test (WONTFIX-keep, already gated by describe.skipIf), (3) packages/testing-real-services/src/postgres-fixture.test.ts:169 already tracked under ra-cit-harness-1. Removed tests/e2e/specs/smoke.spec.ts:140 placeholder (I-230 superseded by login/multi-tenant-isolation/rbac/cernia-ops specs + testcontainers CI). docs/decisions/wontfix-skipped-tests-residual.md documents each resolution.

categories.name: Master Gate (product declared production-ready)
categories.description: All five gates simultaneously true across every wave.

items.title: All 34 + CIT + PROD waves show all 5 gate badges green
items.note: Backend rungs 18/19 green; Admin rungs 7 still ✗; E2E + Conformance gates partial.

items.title: audit-wave-integration.mjs --strict → zero gaps
items.note: 0 blocker backend gaps; admin shelf rungs still flagged.

items.title: pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm test:e2e clean at repo root
items.note: apps/api typecheck clean; per-package suites clean; full repo-root e2e requires Docker stack — runs in CI.

items.title: comprehensive-tests.yml green on main for 7 consecutive days
items.note: Workflow file ships; 7-day window starts the day this branch merges.

items.title: PROD P1 + P2 signed off by compliance officer
items.note: Artifacts ship + committed; signature is operational not code.

honestTotals.literally-code-shipped-rationale: shipped (14 — this session's direct code, including PostgresCarePlanStore + 9 integration tests + the RPM dashboard now end-to-end) + previously-shipped (31 — earlier-session Tasks #1-#210) + shipped-with-stub-followup (7 — heartbeat-only schedulers that boot but await downstream component). All 52 have committed code + verifiable file paths.
honestTotals.wontfix-by-design-rationale: Wave R is intentionally mobile-only — no backend surface needed by design. NOT a deferred item.
honestTotals.still-open-rationale: in-progress (17 — closure-passes + admin UI work + j-* package wiring) + todo (55 — admin UI builds, scheduler stub→real conversion, vendor wiring, deferred-items tracking). These are REAL OPEN WORK, not bookkeeping. v1.2.0 rolled back the v1.1.0 dishonest 100%-done claim where many of these were closed with rolling-WONTFIX framing that hid the fact that the work wasn't done.
honestTotals.note: 52 done + 17 in-progress + 55 todo = 124 total. The previous v1.1.0 claim of 116/116 done was inflated. This Master Punch List now agrees with the Wave Tree (CIT 8/11 in-progress).
